title: Nassella manicata
taxonomy: Nassella manicata is a species of grass in the genus Nassella, which is part of the family Poaceae. The genus Nassella is a group of grasses native to the Americas. Nassella manicata is often classified as a type of bunchgrass. It is commonly known as the Argentine needle grass. Nassella manicata is native to South America and has been introduced to other parts of the world as an invasive species.

intro: Nassella manicata, also known as Argentine Stipa or Fino, is a perennial grass native to South America, characterized by its tall, feathery plumes and delicate foliage.
